task_summary: Panel cohort cell: p2_researcher asks composer to build the schema_contracts_llm_assessment pipeline shape. Generated from panel-broad-2026-05-09.yaml on the panel-broad cohort.
opening_prompt:
I'm working on a medical education assessment project, and what we're trying to understand here is whether we can systematically evaluate clinical reasoning across multiple case studies using consistent criteria. We have a spreadsheet with case material—background information, symptoms, medical history—for two patient scenarios, and we need to score each case against five dimensions: diagnostic accuracy, treatment appropriateness, prognostic assessment, risk identification, and follow-up planning.

The broader question is whether an AI system can apply a structured rubric to these cases reliably enough that we could use the scores and rationales as training feedback for medical students. Rather than having faculty manually review each case against each criterion—which is labour-intensive—I'd like to automate the assessment step so we can focus our time on the qualitative feedback and iteration with learners.

I have the case data in a CSV file with columns for each piece of clinical information. What I need back is: for each case and each of those five assessment dimensions, a numerical score and a written rationale explaining the assessment. Does that make sense? I'm not sure if you can handle multiple evaluation criteria like that in a single pass, or if there's a better way to structure this.

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Lower-case substring match against final assistant content. The skill (anti-passivity section) explicitly forbids these phrases — any hit is RED. Standard list shared with the convergence suite.
red_criteria.build_failure_note: These strings are server-injected by service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() when the model declares completion but the pipeline fails preflight. Their presence is definitive evidence the model failed to converge.
